refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure operational availability meets or exceeds the customer-defined target by minimizing downtime and enabling rapid recovery from faults.
refined_fr.rationale: Availability KPI is decomposed to focus on minimizing downtime and ensuring rapid fault recovery, which are key contributors to operational availability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure system uptime and downtime over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize system downtime through proactive fault detection and mitigation.
FR2: Enable rapid recovery and restoration of system functionality after faults.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement continuous health monitoring and predictive maintenance algorithms.
DP2: Design fault-tolerant system architecture with redundancy and failover capabilities.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement continuous health monitoring and predictive maintenance algorithms.

potential_sources: Interaction between health monitoring and fault recovery mechanisms
Dependency on telemetry data for accurate monitoring
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically supports both sub-FRs with manageable coupling via telemetry interface.

assumptions_risks: Risk of false positives/negatives in predictive maintenance impacting availability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating fault-tolerant architecture (DP2) in next decomposition level for enhanced availability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system-level health monitoring and availability management

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time sensor and status data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends maintenance and recovery task commands to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralized health monitoring fits well within NGHE Solution module boundary.
Partial containment possible but would increase diffusion due to cross-module dependencies.
Lower containment score; health monitoring logic less suited here.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing availability monitoring and management in NGHE Solution minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in NGHE Solution module due to broad scope.
Risk of delayed fault response if interfaces are not timely.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine interface definitions and consider modular subcomponents within NGHE Solution for scalability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP logically satisfies the refined FRs with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some risk of diffusion if health monitoring logic spreads into Vehicle or Teleoperation modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as the best IM boundary for the selected DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and tasking commands is feasible at NGHE Solution level.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of availability management to NGHE Solution module.

recommended_revisions: Further decompose NGHE Solution module in next level to manage complexity.
Clarify interface timing and data quality requirements.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of sub-FRs and DP integration is needed to address fault tolerance explicitly and reduce risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize system downtime through proactive fault detection and mitigation.
FR2: Enable rapid recovery and restoration of system functionality after faults.

revise.why: To explicitly incorporate fault-tolerant design and recovery mechanisms in the functional and design domains.

assumptions: Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
Customer-defined availability target is achievable with health monitoring.
open_risks: False alarms or missed faults in predictive maintenance.
evidence_needed: Simulation or test results of predictive maintenance algorithms.
